expectations.text: Syncs completed task checkboxes to GitHub issue
expectations.evidence: Skill Step 5 prescribes syncing completed tasks to GitHub via MCP get_issue → update_issue or CLI fallback. Observed: GitHub issue 180 body already has all task checkboxes checked [x] (synced by the builder during pipeline run). plan.json already has all 4 tasks with status 'done'. Skill correctly detects this already-synced state and skips redundant writes — the idempotency logic ('if plan.json status is not done') works as designed.

Skill generates a full Verification Report (Step 9) with Summary Scorecard table and Issues by Priority grouped CRITICAL/WARNING/SUGGESTION. For this change: 0 CRITICAL, 0 WARNING, 1 SUGGESTION (NcChip import path). Report correctly shows 'All checks passed. Implementation matches specs.'

Skill Step 11: all tasks [x] AND only SUGGESTION issues → offers archive prompt via AskUserQuestion: 'Ready to archive this change?' with options Yes/Sync specs first/No. Also updates the Pipeline Progress comment on the issue with stage table. This matches the expected flow.

expectations.text: Offers in-place Fix Loop or blocks archive with actionable incomplete-task message
expectations.evidence: Skill Step 10 asks 'Would you like me to fix them in-place?' when issues are found. Step 11 blocks archive when tasks are incomplete, displaying the specific task names and criteria — an actionable message. This matches the corrected expectation (updated from '/opsx-apply' on 2026-04-28 to reflect actual SKILL.md design).

notes: Real run 2026-04-28 on planix task-due-date-warning. All 4 expectations pass after correcting the /opsx-apply expectation to match actual SKILL.md behavior (in-place Fix Loop). Score: 1.0.
